commit.message:
Fix Quick Open file count not updating during scan

When opening Quick Open before scanning completes, the file count in the
bottom bar would get stuck and only update after typing a character.
Added onChange(of: files.count) to force SwiftUI re-render on each batch.

Bump version to 0.4.1 (build 9)

commit.message:
feat(conciliacion): algoritmo inteligente — REPs, nombres SPEI, aprendizaje

- REPs: matching por suma total de pagosPropios (no por pago individual)
  - 1 REP que cubre N facturas ahora matchea correctamente
  - Muestra UUIDs de facturas relacionadas en panel de sugerencias
- Extracción de nombre/RFC de descripciones bancarias (patrones SPEI mexicanos):
  - Egresos: "AL BENEF. NOMBRE" → identifica beneficiario
  - Ingresos: "POR ORDEN DE NOMBRE" → identifica ordenante
  - RFC: detecta formato mexicano en la descripción
- Confidence scoring mejorado:
  - Patrón aprendido → verde automático
  - RFC/nombre en descripción + ≤10 días → verde
  - RFC propio + ≤5 días → verde
- Aprendizaje: al confirmar manualmente → guarda keyword+RFC en reconciliation_patterns
- Cfdi::pagosPropios() — relación correcta para REPs (uuid_pago)
- Migration: tabla reconciliation_patterns
- Orden: verdes primero, REPs antes que PUE, luego por días

commit.message:
Fix email SSL errors, parallelise API calls, and stabilise turn state machine

Backend performance:
- gmail.py: create a fresh httplib2.Http per thread in ThreadPoolExecutor to fix
  SSL WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER errors caused by concurrent threads sharing one Http
- gmail.py, basecamp/tools.py: parallel metadata fetches via ThreadPoolExecutor
- auth.py: use static_discovery=True to eliminate 4s discovery-doc network fetch
- orchestration.py: load MCP servers in background task (non-blocking); reduce
  MCP connect timeout 15s → 5s; add per-tool TIMING + RESULT logging
- api.py: move _upsert_user from execute_tool → health_check (once per session);
  add end-to-end timing log per tool call
- tools/get_time.py: rewrite with DST-aware zoneinfo, city alias map, IANA zones

Frontend state machine (App.tsx):
- handleTurnComplete: skip IDLE transition when tool calls are still in-flight;
  Gemini fires turncomplete immediately after toolcall, before the result arrives
- Revert handleAudioStart guard: guard was unnecessary and caused MODEL_THINKING
  to become a dead-end state, breaking all follow-up conversation after tool calls

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stats 2022 forcees (plus de CMS), galerie design enrichie

- Stats: donnees locales forcees, CMS ne peut plus ecraser (2013->2022)
- Galerie design: ajout affiches events + creations graphiques (12 images)
- Suppression nom de machine dans commentaire products.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move SHL decryption to browser — server never sees decrypted PHI

Architecture change: the entire SHL pipeline (parse URI, fetch manifest,
decrypt JWE, parse FHIR, extract PDFs) now runs in the browser using
jose and pako. The server provides:

- CORS proxy (/api/orgs/:slug/shl-proxy) for encrypted manifest fetches
  with SSRF protection (blocks private IPs, localhost, non-HTTP)
- Route endpoint (/api/orgs/:slug/route) that receives already-decrypted
  data and delivers it to the configured storage destination

The decryption key never leaves the browser. The CORS proxy only ever
handles encrypted JWE blobs. Server-side storage routing (Drive, OneDrive,
Box, Gmail, Outlook, API) is preserved so admins control where data goes
and staff don't need to save files manually.

Also adds Content Security Policy headers, X-Frame-Options, and other
security headers. Updates security spec to v1.1 reflecting the new
client-side decryption architecture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Zero-sort system: global call numbers, per-type libraries, auto-catalog

- Global call number counter (call_counter.db): one atomic sequence across
  all card types and scan modes. Scan order = physical box order. Period.
- Per-type library DBs: MTG (nexus_library.db), Pokemon, Sports, Yu-Gi-Oh
  each get their own library. Existing 26,850 MTG cards untouched.
- Auto-catalog every scan: >=95% auto-accepted, <95% gets placeholder
  call number with needs_review flag for manual correction.
- /api/library/update endpoint: manual review corrects existing entry
  (UPDATE, not duplicate INSERT).
- /api/library/stats?type=all: aggregate stats across all type libraries.
- Settings tab: added Sports Cards (TCDB) game mode radio button.
- Game mode switch updates scanner card_type in real-time (no restart).
- ALTER TABLE migration adds needs_review, confidence columns to existing DBs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Per-project Docker containers with workers as tmux windows (#59) (#59)

Replace the single privileged workspace container with per-project sibling
containers managed by the captain via the host Docker socket. Each project
gets its own isolated container, filesystem, and tmux server. Workers are
now tmux windows inside a project's `agents` session instead of separate
tmux sessions on a shared server.

New scripts: start-project, stop-project, list-projects, create-worker
Removed: launch-worker, setup-project (replaced by above)
Updated: list-workers, capture-worker-output, send-keys-to-worker,
archive-worker (now take PROJECT_NAME parameter)

Voice server and pane monitor dynamically discover project sockets via
PROJECTS_SOCKETS_DIR instead of a fixed WORKSPACE_TMUX_SOCKET.

New SGA players get picked up automatically by the bio enricher.

Fix in-app docs: copy markdown to public/ at build time

The API route using fs.readFileSync fails on Vercel because content/docs/
files aren't bundled into serverless functions. Instead, a prebuild script
copies them to public/docs-content/ as static assets.

commit.message:
Add methods audio button to slide 8 (Hidden Partners / NVDRS)

Web Speech API narration explaining the NLP/ML pipeline for detecting
IPV in pregnancy-associated death narratives:

- NVDRS data source: death certificates + coroner + law enforcement + narratives
- Problem: IPV described in text but not coded as structured variable
- Hand-labeled ~1,500-2,000 narratives with CDC-aligned protocol
- TF-IDF sparse features (unigrams + bigrams) + IPV indicator terms
- Regularized logistic regression & linear SVM classifiers
- Temporal validation: train 2019-2022, test 2023
- Performance benchmarked against human inter-rater kappa

commit.message:
fix(dts): prefer declaration extensions over JS in exports-resolved paths (#1047)

## Summary

- Fix `dts_resolve_esm_match` to try TS/declaration extension
substitution (e.g. `.mjs` → `.d.mts`) **before** checking if the
original JS file exists, matching TypeScript's
`loadFileNameFromPackageJsonField` behavior
- Previously, `resolveDtsSync` would return `.mjs` instead of `.d.mts`
for packages like `magic-string` that have both the JS entry and its
declaration file

Closes #1046

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
fix: rewrite install.sh for cross-machine portability

Root cause: known_marketplaces.json declared marketplace directories
that don't exist on target machines (never git-cloned), causing
Claude Code "scope path" errors on startup.

Changes:
- Remove known_marketplaces.json from export/install entirely
  (let Claude Code manage via /plugin marketplace add)
- Convert go-modern-guidelines from plugin to standalone skill
  (use-modern-go/ works without marketplace infrastructure)
- Remove modern-go-guidelines@go-modern-guidelines from settings.json
- Fix Python inline code: use heredoc + sys.argv instead of
  shell variable interpolation (prevents path injection issues)
- Fix JSON template passing: use stdin pipe instead of triple-quote
  interpolation (prevents quote injection)
- Add rsync to prerequisite checks
- Use $SHELL for RC file detection instead of file existence heuristic
- Only override npm prefix if it's the system default (don't break nvm)
- Use command -v instead of which
- Capture reader-mcp build output to log file
- Add plugins.txt existence check to prevent abort on missing file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: persistent daemon service with OS keychain integration

Add `contextmate daemon install` and `contextmate daemon uninstall` commands
that set up the sync daemon as a persistent OS service (launchd on macOS,
systemd on Linux). Passphrase is stored securely in the OS keychain — no
plaintext files. The daemon auto-starts on login/boot, recovers from crashes,
and restarts on version updates via WatchPaths/PathModified. The setup flow
now offers persistent service installation as the default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor: Use explicit Exception handling instead of bare except

Changed bare 'except:' to 'except Exception:' in three locations:
- Line 201: delete_prim error handling in prim validation
- Line 282: bbox2d_data extraction error handling
- Line 295: cleanup delete_prim error handling

This follows Python best practices (PEP 8) by avoiding bare except
clauses which can catch unexpected exceptions like SystemExit.

commit.message:
fix: exclude MQTT password from backup and device migration (#361)

* fix: exclude MQTT password from backup and device migration

- Backup export: use includeSensitive=false so passwords and API keys
  are not written into the backup zip
- Backup restore: skip all sensitive keys on import (protects against
  old backups that included them)
- Device migration: exclude mqttPassword on settings import

The local settings web UI still allows configuring the MQTT password
on the device itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add comments explaining sensitive key filtering strategy

Clarify why device migration only excludes mqttPassword (other keys
belong to the user) and why backup restore filters on import (older
backups may contain passwords).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

compound: verify error behavior before writing test assertions

Added anti-pattern: "Assuming error behavior without reading code" to
testing-strategy. Added quality check to write-tests: verify error/edge
case assertions match implementation (raises vs returns None vs logs).

Triggered by: integration test assumed method logs warning for missing
record, but implementation raises ValueError — broke CI.
